meaning: vt.每十人杀一人,大批杀害
examples: The pollution could decimate the river's thriving population of kingfishers.
Silber says the tax rollback would decimate basic services for the needy.
Unfortunately, it took the explorers and the settlers who followed only a few decades to decimate a large part of these resources.
Campaigners say a 14-turbine wind farm planned near Selby in North Yorkshire would "decimate" nearby villages.
The recent financial crisis showed us all how a buy-and-hold strategy will decimate your wealth.
